motive: Jealousy over the discovery of the $200K in old coins and a dispute over the grandfather's map.
evidence: The antler trophy on the mantel has been wiped clean — no fingerprints at all, which is suspicious since Marcus handled it regularly.
A small scuff mark on the hardwood floor near the fireplace, consistent with a brief struggle.
Marcus's phone shows his last text at 10:28pm: 'I told you, it's mine now. Deal with it.'
Larry's hiking boots are damp and have fresh mud on them, though he claims he didn't go outside after dinner.
The unlocked back door, though everyone claims it was locked after dinner, suggests someone entered or exited without being noticed.
A pair of rubber gloves from the cleaning supplies has been moved — one is inside-out as if hastily removed, indicating a quick cleanup after the murder.
A journal entry from last week reads: 'Larry's been asking questions. He can't prove anything. The map led me to $200K in old coins — that was MY discovery.'
Diana's lipstick on the coffee mug suggests she was present, but her alibi is weak, indicating she may have been involved or aware of the situation.
